subject: Statistics
title: Meta-Analysis: Combining Studies
description: A fast breakdown of Meta-Analysis: Combining Studies for builders.

hook.text: One study says a drug works. Another says it doesn't. A meta-analysis combines them for a clearer answer.

buildup.text: Meta-analysis pools data from many trials, weighting each by its size and quality.

discovery.text: The combined estimate is more precise than any single study. The forest plot shows each trial's contribution.

twist.text: Garbage in, garbage out. Combining biased studies produces a precisely wrong answer, not a right one.

climax.text: Cochrane Reviews — systematic meta-analyses — are considered the highest level of medical evidence.

punchline.text: One study is a clue. Combined studies are evidence.

quiz.question: What is the purpose of a meta-analysis?
options: To combine results from multiple studies for a more precise and reliable estimate
To replace the need for clinical trials
To find the single best study on a topic
